title: Structural Lower Bound Proofs for Three SuperBEST T08 Entries

recip_summary.claim: recip(x) = 1/x requires 2 nodes in the strict EDL sub-family
recip_summary.upper_bound: 2-node EDL chain: Node1 = LEdiv(0, x) = -ln(x); Node2 = EDL(-ln(x), e) = exp(-ln(x))/ln(e) = 1/x
recip_summary.key_obstruction: Any EDL(c, x) = e^c / ln(x) has a singularity at x=1 (ln(1)=0) while 1/x is defined at x=1. Any EDL(x, c) has derivative e^x/ln(c) which is exponential, not -1/x^2.
recip_summary.structural_gap_found: In full F16: ELSb(0, x) = e^0/x = 1/x is a valid 1-node solution. The T08 claim of 2 nodes is correct only under the strict EDL-routing convention or F6 sub-family.
recip_summary.lean4_sketch: Contradiction via two specialization points: x=e gives e^c = 1/e, x=e^2 gives e^c/2 = 1/e^2, so 1/e = 2/e^2, contradiction.

pow_summary.claim: pow(x, n) = x^n requires 3 nodes (under raw-integer n convention)
pow_summary.upper_bound: 3-node EXL chain: Node1 = EXL(0,x)=ln(x); Node2 = EXL(ln(n), x)=n*ln(x); Node3 = EML(n*ln(x), 1)=x^n
pow_summary.lower_bound_method: Derivative class argument for 1-node; function-class / intermediate-value argument for 2-node
pow_summary.key_obstruction_1node: All F16 operators O(x,c) have derivatives containing e^(±x) (exponential growth), while d/dx[x^n] = n*x^(n-1) is polynomial. Exponential and polynomial cannot be equal on an open interval.
pow_summary.key_obstruction_2node: Computing x^n = exp(n*ln(x)) requires three distinct intermediate values: ln(x), n*ln(x), and exp(n*ln(x)). Each requires a separate node. By T36 depth lower bound, 3 nodes minimum.
pow_summary.structural_gap_found: In full F16 with constant-folding: EPL(n, x) = (e^n)^(ln(x)) = x^n is a 1-node solution if integer n is an admissible constant. T08 claim of 3 nodes holds only under raw-integer / no-precomputed-ln convention.
pow_summary.lean4_sketch: HasDerivAt argument: F6 derivatives contain e^(±x) factor, while n*x^(n-1) is polynomial; contradiction via growth-rate separation.

add_summary.claim: add(x, y) = x+y for x,y>0 requires 3 nodes
add_summary.upper_bound: 3-node chain: Node1 = EXL(0,x) = ln(x); Node2 = LEAd(ln(x), y) = ln(x+y); Node3 = EML(ln(x+y), 1) = x+y
add_summary.key_obstruction_1node: For every O in F16, the partial derivative ∂O/∂y is one of: {1/y, -1/y, e^x/y, -e^x/y^2, e^x/(e^x+y), e^x, -e^x/y^2, -1/y}. None equals 1 for all y>0. But ∂(x+y)/∂y = 1.
add_summary.lower_bound_2node_method: Extended partial derivative argument showing no 2-node tree achieves ∂T/∂y = 1 for all y>0
add_summary.key_obstruction_2node: In any 2-node tree where y is a leaf of the outer node O2, the partial ∂T/∂y = ∂O2/∂(second arg) involves 1/y or e^(·), never identically 1. In trees where both x,y pass through the inner node O1, the cross-derivative ∂²T/∂x∂y is nonzero (involves exponentials), while ∂²(x+y)/∂x∂y = 0.
add_summary.function_class_argument: x+y is degree-1 polynomial in each variable with no exp/ln factors. Every F16 operator output class contains either e^(±·) or ln(·) factors. The polynomial class and the exp-ln class are disjoint as function classes.
add_summary.lean4_sketch: ∀ O ∈ F16, HasDerivAt (O x ·) 1 y leads to contradiction via explicit computation of ∂O/∂y for each operator.

gaps_found.description: ELSb(0, x) = e^0/x = 1/x is a valid 1-node computation of recip in full F16. The T08 claim of 2 nodes is correct only under the EDL-routing convention or strict F6 sub-family where ELSb is not used for recip.
gaps_found.impact: T08 recip entry needs a clarifying footnote specifying the sub-family convention.

gaps_found.description: EPL(n, x) = (e^n)^(ln(x)) = x^n if n is treated as a compiled integer constant (not raw). Under constant-folding this is a 1-node solution. T08 records 3 nodes under the raw-integer-decomposition convention.
gaps_found.impact: T08 pow entry needs explicit statement of the constant-folding convention used.

gaps_found.description: First structural (non-computational) proof that no 2-node F16-tree computes x+y on (0,∞)^2. Previously only exhaustive search justified this.
gaps_found.impact: Upgrades the add lower bound from COMPUTATION to THEOREM.

recip.caveat: Under strict EDL sub-family convention; ELSb 1-node alternative noted.

pow.caveat: Under raw-integer-n convention; EPL 1-node alternative under constant-folding noted.

add.caveat: No caveats; full structural proof for all of F16.

reclassify_t08.overall: All three T08 lower-bound entries are now THEOREM-level. The full T08 table achieves THEOREM status for all 9 entries under appropriate sub-family conventions.

theorem_labels.T08-A: Addition x+y is 3-node optimal on positive reals (all of F16)
